verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is canonically a secreted serine protease with no TM domain or GPI anchor. It has historically been classified as secreted-only. However, rigorous 2025 data (FACS, confocal co-localization with EpCAM, patient-derived tumor cells) confirm cell-surface expression in AR+ prostate cancer cells. Multiple cell-surface-directed therapeutics (CAR-T, KLK2×CD3 BiTE in Phase I, 225Ac-RLT) engage this surface pool. Expression is highly prostate/prostate-cancer-specific with little-to-no surface expression elsewhere. The mechanism of surface retention is not fully characterized but the surface pool is restricted to a narrow prostate cancer lineage context, fitting tissue_restricted_surface (prostate cancer cells).
